name: Benny Powell
abstract: Benjamin Gordon Powell Jr. (March 1, 1930 – June 26, 2010) was an American jazz trombonist.

interviews.title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: A.B. Spellman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I had the good fortune to interview the trombone player Benny Powell. And he said in his opinion no great art ever came from happiness...
about_quotes.doc_title: A.B. Spellman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: Benny studied with me for a couple of years. We got to be really good friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Powell was a very dear friend of mine. We was in Basie's band together.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Powell and I, being pretty much in the same age group, formed a sort of alliance of youngsters in the band. ... I used him on the first recording I did under my own name, Here Comes Frank Foster.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: But, in 1996, I then started along with Benny Powell, Jameel Nasser, and Bob Cranshaw. It was the jazz committee and Benny Powell named the jazz advisory committee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was involved with something called the American Jazz Orchestra... And this was a band comprised of mostly big band veterans: ... Benny Powell
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019
